rows.courseDescription: Understanding quarterly estimated taxes, self-employment tax, deductible expenses, and tax planning strategies for independent contractors.
Creating professional invoices, payment terms, tracking receivables, and using the L.A.W.S. Business OS invoicing system.
Reading and negotiating contracts, key clauses to watch for, liability limitations, and intellectual property considerations.
Legal distinctions between IC and employee status, IRS classification tests, maintaining proper IC relationship, avoiding misclassification.
Choosing the right business structure (LLC, S-Corp, etc.), state registration requirements, operating agreements, and compliance.
Opening business accounts, separating personal and business finances, bookkeeping basics, and financial record keeping.
General liability, professional liability, workers comp considerations, and insurance requirements for contractors.
Complete walkthrough of the L.A.W.S. Business OS platform including invoicing, contract management, and network features.
